prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(receive_and_register_batch inbound_batch_1)
(receive_and_register_batch outbound_batch_1)
(receive_and_register_batch fumigation_chamber_a)
(receive_and_register_batch fumigation_chamber_b)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_batch inbound_batch_1 schedule_slot_1)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_batch outbound_batch_1 schedule_slot_2)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_batch fumigation_chamber_a schedule_slot_3)
(conduct_quality_test fumigation_chamber_a quality_test_device_1)
(record_quality_clearance fumigation_chamber_a quality_test_device_1)
(assign_technician_to_batch fumigation_chamber_a technician_1)
(assign_monitoring_instrument_to_chamber fumigation_chamber_a monitoring_instrument_1)
(apply_safety_protocol_to_chamber fumigation_chamber_a safety_protocol_1 quality_test_device_1)
(complete_safety_checks_for_chamber fumigation_chamber_a technician_1)
(assign_monitoring_instrument_post_safety fumigation_chamber_a monitoring_instrument_1)
(finalize_safety_approval_and_mark_ready fumigation_chamber_a)
(schedule_release_window_for_batch fumigation_chamber_a release_window_1 quality_test_device_1)
(finalize_release_for_chamber_and_free_slot fumigation_chamber_a schedule_slot_3 release_window_1)
(release_quality_test_device fumigation_chamber_a quality_test_device_1)
(unassign_technician_from_batch fumigation_chamber_a technician_1)
(unassign_monitoring_instrument_from_chamber fumigation_chamber_a monitoring_instrument_1)
(reserve_schedule_slot_for_batch fumigation_chamber_b schedule_slot_3)
(conduct_quality_test inbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
(record_quality_clearance inbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
(stage_pallet_for_batch inbound_batch_1 treatment_profile_1 pallet_1)
(finalize_pallet_staging_for_batch inbound_batch_1 treatment_profile_1 quality_test_device_1 pallet_1)
(release_quality_test_device inbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
(conduct_quality_test outbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
(record_quality_clearance outbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
(stage_pallet_for_outbound outbound_batch_1 receiving_profile_1 pallet_1)
(finalize_pallet_staging_for_outbound_batch outbound_batch_1 receiving_profile_1 quality_test_device_1 pallet_1)
(release_quality_test_device outbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
(allocate_container_for_consolidation inbound_batch_1 outbound_batch_1 treatment_profile_1 receiving_profile_1 container_unit_1)
(conduct_quality_test inbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
(verify_container_loading container_unit_1 inbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
(release_quality_test_device inbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
(finalize_inbound_batch_release inbound_batch_1 container_unit_1)
(finalize_outbound_batch_release outbound_batch_1 container_unit_1)
(conduct_quality_test fumigation_chamber_b quality_test_device_1)
(record_quality_clearance fumigation_chamber_b quality_test_device_1)
(attach_inspection_record_to_container fumigation_chamber_b inspection_record_2 container_unit_1)
(confirm_inspection_and_enable_chamber fumigation_chamber_b inspection_record_2 container_unit_1 quality_test_device_1)
(allocate_fumigant_supply_to_chamber fumigation_chamber_b fumigant_supply_1)
(assign_monitoring_instrument_to_chamber fumigation_chamber_b monitoring_instrument_1)
(assign_technician_to_batch fumigation_chamber_b technician_1)
(stage_fumigant_into_chamber fumigation_chamber_b fumigant_supply_1 technician_1 inspection_record_2 container_unit_1)
(configure_treatment_monitoring fumigation_chamber_b monitoring_instrument_1 inspection_record_2 container_unit_1)
(finalize_chamber_and_mark_ready fumigation_chamber_b)
(schedule_release_window_for_batch fumigation_chamber_b release_window_1 quality_test_device_1)
(finalize_release_for_chamber_and_free_slot fumigation_chamber_b schedule_slot_3 release_window_1)
(release_quality_test_device fumigation_chamber_b quality_test_device_1)
(unassign_technician_from_batch fumigation_chamber_b technician_1)
(unassign_monitoring_instrument_from_chamber fumigation_chamber_b monitoring_instrument_1)
(release_fumigant_supply_from_chamber fumigation_chamber_b fumigant_supply_1)
(conduct_quality_test inbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
(schedule_release_window_for_batch inbound_batch_1 release_window_1 quality_test_device_1)
(finalize_release_for_inbound_batch_and_free_slot inbound_batch_1 schedule_slot_1 release_window_1)
(release_quality_test_device inbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
(conduct_quality_test outbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
(schedule_release_window_for_batch outbound_batch_1 release_window_1 quality_test_device_1)
(finalize_release_for_outbound_batch_and_free_slot outbound_batch_1 schedule_slot_2 release_window_1)
(release_quality_test_device outbound_batch_1 quality_test_device_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for fumigation chamber turnover scheduling. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Understanding the Task  
- The problem focuses on fumigation chamber turnover scheduling using PDDL (Planning Domain Definition Language).  
- The goal is to generate an executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions.  
- Each action must be formatted as (action-name arg1 arg2 ...) and listed one per line.  
- The output should only include the final plan, without additional explanations or domain details.

## Identifying Goals  
- The goal is to achieve turnover_complete for inbound_batch_1, outbound_batch_1, fumigation_chamber_a, and fumigation_chamber_b.  
- All four items (two batches, two chambers) must go through the full workflow, including intake, scheduling, quality approval, staging, treatment, and release.  

## Examining Initial Conditions  
- Initially, no batches or chambers are registered, intake_registered, or quality_approved.  
- Resources are limited, with one quality_test_device_1, technician_1, pallet_1, container_unit_1, release_window_1, and fumigant_supply_1 available.  

## Exploring Workflow Steps  
- Start by registering all workflow items using receive_and_register_batch for inbound_batch_1, outbound_batch_1, fumigation_chamber_a, and fumigation_chamber_b.  
- Chambers, treated as workflow_items, also need quality tests and approvals, similar to batches.  

## Managing Resource Constraints  
- Only three scheduling slots (schedule_slot_1,2,3) are available, but slots can be reused after turnover completion.  
- Quality tests require careful sequencing due to limited resources like one quality_test_device_1 and technician_1, with actions like conduct_quality_test and record_quality_clearance needed before releasing devices.

## Managing Resource Constraints  
- Scheduling requires intake_registered and slot_available, leading to scheduled_for_fumigation and assigned_slot, with slots reusable after turnover completion.  
- Quality testing needs intake_registered, scheduled_for_fumigation, and quality_test_available, transitioning to tested_with, with the device reusable after release.  

## Sequencing Workflow Steps  
- All four items (batches and chambers) must be registered, scheduled, quality tested, cleared, and reach ready_for_release before final release.  
- Release scheduling uses one reusable window, requiring careful sequencing to manage limited resources like the single container_unit_1.  

## Analyzing Container Allocation  
- Container allocation is critical for batches, requiring batch_staged and container_loading_verified, but only one container_unit_1 is available with no deallocation action.  
- Chambers also need container allocation for treatment monitoring, linking inspection records, suggesting shared resource challenges for concurrent processing.

## Examining Safety Protocol Limitations  
- Only one safety_protocol_2 is available, and no release action exists, suggesting only one chamber can use the safety path.  
- The goal requires both chambers to reach turnover_complete, which may conflict with limited safety protocol availability.

## Investigating Chamber Workflow Alternatives  
- Chambers cannot reach treatment_monitoring_configured without linking to a container via inspection_record_linked_to_container, as all configure_treatment_monitoring actions require it.  
- No alternative actions set treatment_monitoring_configured, limiting chamber processing options without container allocation.

## Assessing Batch and Chamber Resource Sharing  
- Batches require container allocation for ready_for_release, with finalize_batch_release actions needing container_allocated and container_loading_verified.  
- With only one container_unit_1, it can handle paired inbound and outbound batches, but chambers face resource conflicts for concurrent processing.

## Mapping Batch Sequencing

- Both inbound and outbound batches need pallet staging and finalization, using one pallet sequentially, with finalize_pallet_staging setting profile_reserved for treatment and receiving profiles.  
- The process involves stage_pallet_for_batch for inbound and outbound, then finalize_pallet_staging_for_batch, ensuring both batches are batch_staged and ready_for_treatment before container allocation.  

## Detailing Container Allocation Steps

- Allocate_container_for_consolidation requires both batches ready_for_treatment, profile_reserved, and container_available, using container_unit_1 for consolidation.  
- Effects include container_allocated and container_assigned_to_treatment_profile, enabling further processing with shared resources.

## Exploring Chamber Safety Paths  
- Chambers need registration, scheduling, quality testing, and approval before safety protocols can be applied.  
- Quality_test_device_1 is needed for testing, and it can be released after recording quality clearance and finalizing pallet staging.  

## Managing Resource Constraints  
- Only one safety_protocol_1 is available initially, limiting safety protocol application to chamber_a; chamber_b lacks access to safety_protocol_2.  
- Technician_1 and monitoring_instrument_1 are required for safety checks, with no release actions, suggesting sequential processing for chambers.  

## Investigating Alternative Chamber Processing  
- Chamber_b cannot use the safety path due to unavailability of safety_protocol_2, requiring exploration of other processing options.  
- Container-based paths for chambers require container allocation, which is already tied to batches, posing a resource conflict.

## Examining Chamber Processing Paths  
- Chambers can reach ready_for_release via safety paths or container-based paths, both requiring fumigant deployment and monitoring configuration.  
- Container-based paths involve attaching carriers, consolidating outputs, and finalizing, needing compatible batches and prior staging.  

## Assessing Resource Availability  
- Safety protocol availability limits processing: only safety_protocol_1 is available initially, restricting chamber_a to the safety path.  
- Chamber_b cannot use the safety path due to unavailability of safety_protocol_2, requiring alternative container-based processing.  

## Exploring Resource Sharing  
- Batches and chambers share resources like containers and fumigants, potentially allowing concurrent processing if staged sequentially.  
- Achieving turnover_complete for both chambers may require alternating between safety and container paths, balancing resource constraints.  

## Checking Compliance Requirements  
- Monitoring compliance must be confirmed for some paths, with compliance certificates needed for carrier attachment and consolidation.  
- Treatment monitoring configuration is a prerequisite for finalizing chambers, ensuring quality and safety standards are met.

## Evaluating Chamber and Batch Processing  
- Chambers and batches might share the consolidation path, processing both together by loading containers into chambers, fumigating, and finalizing.  
- Finalizing consolidation could mark both chambers and batches as ready_for_release, but resource sharing complicates this for two chambers with one container.  

## Assessing Resource Reuse  
- After turnover_complete, no actions free up containers or carriers, limiting reuse for the second chamber.  
- Safety paths don't require containers, but the lack of safety_protocol_2 for chamber_b restricts this option.  

## Exploring Container Allocation Variants  
- Allocate_container_with_inbound_pallets allows staging without finalizing pallets, requiring inbound batches staged and outbound ready.  
- This variant needs receiving_profile_reserved and container_available, potentially easing resource constraints for processing.

## Analyzing Container and Chamber Sharing  
- Multiple inspection records can be attached to the same container, allowing different chambers to share it for processing.  
- Preconditions for attaching records, like quality_approved and container_loading_verified, don't prevent multiple chambers using one container.  

## Checking Inspection and Monitoring Processes  
- Each chamber can confirm inspection and configure monitoring separately, even with shared resources like containers.  
- Monitoring instruments and fumigant supplies can be assigned, used, and unassigned between chambers, enabling sequential processing.  

## Evaluating Consolidation Feasibility  
- Consolidating outputs into a shared container is possible if both chambers are compatible and monitoring compliance is confirmed.  
- This approach requires batches to be ready and treatment monitoring configured for each chamber individually.

## Investigating Container Loading Checks  
- Container loading checks (a and b) are set by specific allocate_container variants, like with inbound or outbound pallets, but not with the for_consolidation variant.  
- Using allocate_container_for_consolidation, which requires both profiles reserved and batches ready, avoids setting these checks, enabling flexible processing.  

## Examining Fumigant and Monitoring Paths  
- Stage_fumigant_into_chamber requires no container_loading_check_b and a technician assigned, allowing fumigant deployment without compliance checks.  
- Configure_treatment_monitoring sets treatment_monitoring_configured without needing monitoring_compliance_confirmed, enabling chamber finalization.  

## Optimizing for Multiple Chambers  
- Both chambers can share the container if allocated for consolidation, avoiding loading checks and allowing sequential processing.  
- This approach ensures quality_approved status for each chamber, supporting shared resource use without safety protocol dependency.

## Optimizing for Multiple Chambers  
- Multiple batches can share the same quality test device (qt), with each batch maintaining a tested_with relationship until needed.  
- Actions like verify_container_loading and confirm_inspection require tested_with to remain active, ensuring sequential processing works for shared qt use.  

## Addressing Quality Test Device Conflicts  
- The quality test device can only be used sequentially, requiring release of tested_with for one batch before testing another, creating a conflict.  
- Releasing the device removes tested_with for the previous batch, but actions later in the sequence still require tested_with, making simultaneous use impossible.

## Examining Process Sequencing  
- To process inbound_1, register, schedule, and conduct quality tests first, setting tested_with i1 and making qt unavailable.  
- After recording and approving i1, pallet staging and finalizing for i1 require tested_with i1, but qt remains unavailable for outbound_1.

## Analyzing Outbound Processing Challenges  
- Outbound_1 requires conducting quality tests, but with only one qt, it’s unavailable after inbound_1, creating a sequential conflict.  
- Finalizing pallet staging for outbound needs tested_with o1, and releasing qt removes tested_with o1, blocking later actions like schedule_release.

## Exploring Alternative Staging Paths  
- Using reserve_treatment_profile_for_batch and assign_technician_to_batch for inbound can set batch_ready_for_treatment without pallet staging, relying on tested_with.  
- This technician-based approach avoids pallet-related tested_with requirements, potentially easing sequential qt use.

## Extending Inbound Processing Steps  
- Conducting quality tests for inbound_batch_1 with quality_test_device_1 sets tested_with i1, making qt unavailable.  
- Recording clearance and reserving treatment profile for inbound_batch_1 requires tested_with i1, maintaining qt unavailability.  

## Finalizing Inbound Staging  
- Assigning technician_1 to inbound_batch_1 and preparing the chamber sets batch_staged and batch_ready for i1, still holding tested_with i1.  
- Unassigning the technician makes technician_1 available again, but qt remains unavailable due to tested_with i1.  

## Identifying Outbound Testing Conflicts  
- Outbound_batch_1 cannot start testing as qt is unavailable, stuck until inbound_batch_1 releases it.  
- Releasing qt for inbound_batch_1 removes tested_with i1, but later actions like verify_container_loading still require it.  

## Analyzing Sequential Processing Limits  
- Both inbound and outbound batches need tested_with until late stages like schedule_release, conflicting with single qt use.  
- Releasing qt removes tested_with, making it impossible to test multiple batches simultaneously while maintaining needed relationships.

## Proposing Retesting Strategy  
- Retesting can address qt unavailability by conducting quality tests again before actions like verify or schedule_release, resetting tested_with as needed.  
- For example, conduct tests for inbound, perform staging, release qt, then retest before verify or schedule_release to maintain required states.  

## Outlining Pallet-Based Sequencing  
- Using pallet staging avoids technician conflicts, as stage_pallet and finalize_pallet_staging don’t require technician assignment, freeing t1 for chambers.  
- Process inbound_batch_1 and outbound_batch_1 sequentially using pallet_1, releasing it after each finalize, ensuring no resource conflicts.  

## Detailing Initial Processing Steps  
- Register all batches and chambers, then reserve schedule slots for inbound_batch_1, outbound_batch_1, and fumigation_chamber_a initially.  
- Conduct and record quality tests for inbound_batch_1, then stage and finalize pallet staging, releasing qt afterward for next use.  

## Continuing Outbound Processing  
- After inbound, conduct and record quality tests for outbound_batch_1, then stage and finalize pallet staging using the now-available pallet.  
- Release qt after outbound staging, ensuring both batches are approved and ready for later actions without qt conflicts.

## Managing Chamber Release Process  
- Chamber_a is ready for release after marking it ready, requiring a release window and tested_with qt.  
- Schedule release for chamber_a using quality_test_device_1, freeing slot3 and release_window_1 after finalizing.  

## Handling Resource Availability Post-Release  
- After scheduling release, qt can be released, making it available for other batches since tested_a is no longer needed.  
- Technician_1 and monitoring_instrument (mi) remain assigned to chamber_a post-safety checks; unassigning them is required to reuse for other tasks.  

## Ensuring Technician and MI Reusability  
- Unassigning technician_1 from chamber_a is possible anytime after assignment, setting it available for new tasks.  
- Similarly, unassigning mi from chamber_a after safety checks ensures it’s available, given only one mi is in use.  

## Preparing for Chamber B Processing  
- After freeing resources, slot3 is available for scheduling chamber_b, continuing the sequence post-chamber_a turnover.  
- Earlier, slots for inbound_batch_1 (slot1) and outbound_batch_1 (slot2) were already reserved, aligning with the planned flow.

## Assigning Resources for Chamber B  
- Attach inspection_record_2 (ir2) to fumigation_chamber_b, linking it with container_unit_1, making ir2 unavailable.  
- Assign fumigant_supply_1, monitoring_instrument_1, and technician_1 to chamber_b, ensuring all are available from prior unassignments.  

## Staging and Configuring Chamber B  
- Stage fumigant into chamber_b using fumigant_supply_1 and technician_1, verifying chamber preparation and inspection linkage.  
- Configure treatment monitoring for chamber_b with monitoring_instrument_1, ensuring fumigant is deployed and inspections are linked.  

## Finalizing and Releasing Chamber B  
- Finalize chamber_b and mark it ready for release, noting monitoring compliance is not yet confirmed.  
- Schedule release for chamber_b using release_window_1 and quality_test_device_1, ensuring it’s tested and slot3 is assigned.  

## Managing Resource Release Post-Chamber B  
- Unassign technician_1, monitoring_instrument_1, and release fumigant_supply_1 from chamber_b to make them available for other tasks.  
- Note quality_test_device_1 remains unavailable after chamber_b’s conduct, requiring release before scheduling others.

## Scheduling Chamber A Release  
- Schedule release window for chamber_a using release_window_1 and quality_test_device_1, confirming it’s ready and tested.  
- Finalize release for chamber_a, freeing schedule_slot_3 and release_window_1, completing turnover.  

## Releasing Chamber A Resources  
- Release quality_test_device_1 from chamber_a, making it available for subsequent tasks.  
- Unassign technician_1 and monitoring_instrument_1 from chamber_a, freeing them for reuse.

## Examining Process Sequencing  
- To process inbound_1, register, schedule, and conduct quality tests first, setting tested_with i1 and making qt unavailable.  
- After recording and approving i1, pallet staging and finalizing for i1 require tested_with i1, but qt remains unavailable for outbound_1.

## Analyzing Outbound Processing Challenges  
- Outbound_1 requires conducting quality tests, but with only one qt, it’s unavailable after inbound_1, creating a sequential conflict.  
- Finalizing pallet staging for outbound needs tested_with o1, and releasing qt removes tested_with o1, blocking later actions like schedule_release.

## Exploring Alternative Staging Paths  
- Using reserve_treatment_profile_for_batch and assign_technician_to_batch for inbound can set batch_ready_for_treatment without pallet staging, relying on tested_with.  
- This technician-based approach avoids pallet-related tested_with requirements, potentially easing sequential qt use.

## Extending Inbound Processing Steps  
- Conducting quality tests for inbound_batch_1 with quality_test_device_1 sets tested_with i1, making qt unavailable.  
- Recording clearance and reserving treatment profile for inbound_batch_1 requires tested_with i1, maintaining qt unavailability.  

## Finalizing Inbound Staging  
- Assigning technician_1 to inbound_batch_1 and preparing the chamber sets batch_staged and batch_ready for i1, still holding tested_with i1.  
- Unassigning the technician makes technician_1 available again, but qt remains unavailable due to tested_with i1.  

## Identifying Outbound Testing Conflicts  
- Outbound_batch_1 cannot start testing as qt is unavailable, stuck until inbound_batch_1 releases it.  
- Releasing qt for inbound_batch_1 removes tested_with i1, but later actions like verify_container_loading still require it.  

## Analyzing Sequential Processing Limits  
- Both inbound and outbound batches need tested_with until late stages like schedule_release, conflicting with single qt use.  
- Releasing qt removes tested_with, making it impossible to test multiple batches simultaneously while maintaining needed relationships.

## Proposing Retesting Strategy  
- Retesting can address qt unavailability by conducting quality tests again before actions like verify or schedule_release, resetting tested_with as needed.  
- For example, conduct tests for inbound, perform staging, release qt, then retest before verify or schedule_release to maintain required states.  

## Outlining Pallet-Based Sequencing  
- Using pallet staging avoids technician conflicts, as stage_pallet and finalize_pallet_staging don’t require technician assignment, freeing t1 for chambers.  
- Process inbound_batch_1 and outbound_batch_1 sequentially using pallet_1, releasing it after each finalize, ensuring no resource conflicts.  

## Detailing Initial Processing Steps  
- Register all batches and chambers, then reserve schedule slots for inbound_batch_1, outbound_batch_1, and fumigation_chamber_a initially.  
- Conduct and record quality tests for inbound_batch_1, then stage and finalize pallet staging, releasing qt afterward for next use.  

## Continuing Outbound Processing  
- After inbound, conduct and record quality tests for outbound_batch_1, then stage and finalize pallet staging using the now-available pallet.  
- Release qt after outbound staging, ensuring both batches are approved and ready for later actions without qt conflicts.

## Managing Chamber Release Process  
- Chamber_a is ready for release after marking it ready, requiring a release window and tested_with qt.  
- Schedule release for chamber_a using quality_test_device_1, freeing slot3 and release_window_1 after finalizing.  

## Handling Resource Availability Post-Release  
- After scheduling release, qt can be released, making it available for other batches since tested_a is no longer needed.  
- Technician_1 and monitoring_instrument (mi) remain assigned to chamber_a post-safety checks; unassigning them is required to reuse for other tasks.  

## Ensuring Technician and MI Reusability  
- Unassigning technician_1 from chamber_a is possible anytime after assignment, setting it available for new tasks.  
- Similarly, unassigning mi from chamber_a after safety checks ensures it’s available, given only one mi is in use.  

## Preparing for Chamber B Processing  
- After freeing resources, slot3 is available for scheduling chamber_b, continuing the sequence post-chamber_a turnover.  
- Earlier, slots for inbound_batch_1 (slot1) and outbound_batch_1 (slot2) were already reserved, aligning with the planned flow.

## Assigning Resources for Chamber B  
- Attach inspection_record_2 (ir2) to fumigation_chamber_b, linking it with container_unit_1, making ir2 unavailable.  
- Assign fumigant_supply_1, monitoring_instrument_1, and technician_1 to chamber_b, ensuring all are available from prior unassignments.  

## Staging and Configuring Chamber B  
- Stage fumigant into chamber_b using fumigant_supply_1 and technician_1, verifying chamber preparation and inspection linkage.  
- Configure treatment monitoring for chamber_b with monitoring_instrument_1, ensuring fumigant is deployed and inspections are linked.  

## Finalizing and Releasing Chamber B  
- Finalize chamber_b and mark it ready for release, noting monitoring compliance is not yet confirmed.  
- Schedule release for chamber_b using release_window_1 and quality_test_device_1, ensuring it’s tested and slot3 is assigned.  

## Managing Resource Release Post-Chamber B  
- Unassign technician_1, monitoring_instrument_1, and release fumigant_supply_1 from chamber_b to make them available for other tasks.  
- Note quality_test_device_1 remains unavailable after chamber_b’s conduct, requiring release before scheduling others.

## Scheduling Chamber A Release  
- Schedule release window for chamber_a using release_window_1 and quality_test_device_1, confirming it’s ready and tested.  
- Finalize release for chamber_a, freeing schedule_slot_3 and release_window_1, completing turnover.  

## Releasing Chamber A Resources  
- Release quality_test_device_1 from chamber_a, making it available for subsequent tasks.  
- Unassign technician_1 and monitoring_instrument_1 from chamber_a, freeing them for reuse.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
